commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7f8bee4f2e1dff5abf9ea37458721ba4b2310c5f)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds session-store and first-user-message guards around message-level `[cron:]` archive classification in `buildSessionEntry` and adds regression coverage for a human `[cron:]` reset archive after prior conversation.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +53. Total +61 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main and this PR’s first-user path can classify a usage-counted archive as cron-generated from user text, clear collected content, and skip later messages before memory sync indexes the entry.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs maintainer judgment on the trusted archive cron-provenance boundary before an automated repair can safely choose between first-turn human recall and metadata-less cron opacity.

target.title: fix(memory): stop message-level [cron: classification from wiping non-cron archives (fixes #98241) (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a user message in a session transcript starts with `[cron:...]` (e.g., pasting a cron log line, or asking "[cron:daily-digest] why did it fail?"), the `buildSessionEntry` function in `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.ts` incorrectly classifies the entire archive as cron-generated. This wipes all indexed content from that archive, making `memory_search` unable to find any previously remembered facts from the session — even facts unrelated to the `[cron:` message.

The root cause is a message-level `isGeneratedCronPromptMessage` check that matches human-typed text patterns, contradicting the safety comment at line 855-861 of the same file which warns: "user-typed text can match those same patterns (`[cron:...]`) and a cross-message drop would let users exfiltrate real assistant replies from the dreaming corpus by prefixing their own prompt."

## Why This Change Was Made

The message-level `[cron:]` classification fires on any user message matching `/^\[cron:[^\]]+\]\s*/`, regardless of whether the session is actually cron-generated. For usage-counted archives (`.reset` / `.deleted`), this incorrectly:
1. Sets `generatedByCronRun = true`
2. Wipes all previously collected content (`collected.length = 0`)
3. Skips all remaining messages

The fix adds two guards to the message-level check:
- `!hasSeenPriorUserMessage`: Only treat `[cron:` as a cron prompt when it's the first user message (real cron sessions start with the cron prompt; human sessions have prior conversation)
- `!sessionStoreAlreadyClassifiedCron`: Skip when the session store already has authoritative cron classification

This preserves opacity for orphan cron-run archives (where `[cron:` IS the first user message) while fixing the false positive for human-typed `[cron:` in non-cron sessions.

## User Impact

Users who paste cron log lines or ask about cron jobs in their sessions will no longer lose all their session memory after a reset or delete. The `[cron:` prefixed user message is still stripped by `sanitizeSessionText`, but the surrounding conversation and remembered facts stay indexed.

## Evidence

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `buildSessionEntry` incorrectly classifies human-typed `[cron:]` user messages as cron-generated in usage-counted archives, wiping all indexed content.
- **Environment tested:** macOS, Node v22.22.3, OpenClaw 2026.5.28, `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.ts` from branch `fix/memory-session-archive-cron-classification`.
- **Steps run after the patch:** Ran the production `buildSessionEntry` function via `tsx` with two synthetic session transcripts: (1) a reset archive where a human types `[cron:]` after prior conversation, and (2) an orphan cron archive where `[cron:]` is the first user message.
- **Evidence after fix:**

```
=== Real behavior proof: buildSessionEntry (production function) ===

Scenario 1: Human-typed [cron:] after prior conversation
  Content has Acme Robotics: ✅ YES
  Content has digest answer: ✅ YES
  generatedByCronRun: false
  Content lines: 5

Scenario 2: Orphan cron archive (first message is [cron:])
  Content empty (opaque): ✅ YES
  generatedByCronRun: true

✅ ALL SCENARIOS PASS
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Human-typed `[cron:]` content in non-cron reset archives is preserved and indexed. Orphan cron archives (first message is `[cron:]`) remain opaque. The `buildSessionEntry` production function correctly distinguishes between the two cases using the first-user-message heuristic.
- **What was not tested:** Live `memory_search` queries against a running gateway (no embedding provider available in this environment). The proof exercises the exact production code path (`buildSessionEntry`) that feeds the memory index.

- [x] AI-assisted (Hermes Agent)
